attacking_style: Not a primary attacker; operates as a precision domestique delivering leaders to specific launch points (e.g., 6.5km mark at Tour de Suisse). When racing for himself, shows ability to survive into finale selection groups rather than initiate attacks.
team_tactics: Deployed as tempo-setter on climbs and positioning controller on flat stages. Shares pacemaking duties with other domestiques (observed with Masnada) to extend gaps in final 15km. Teams trust him for technical corner navigation and GC group management.
terrain_preference: Versatile climber who excels in controlled tempo scenarios rather than explosive gradients. Capable in crosswinds and technical finales. Functions best in stage races requiring sustained positioning effort rather than single-day punch.
vulnerability: Not equipped for explosive attacks or pure sprint finishes. Role limits offensive opportunities. Effectiveness depends on team strength—performance declined when Ineos squad reduced to three riders in 2019 Vuelta.
key_patterns: Consistently used as pacemaker in final 15km to control GC groups (3 observations)
Exceeds domestique expectations by making final selection groups with GC contenders (3 observations)
Demonstrates crosswind positioning skills, making splits beyond climbing specialist profile (2 observations)
Trusted for technical course navigation and flat-stage positioning control (2 observations)
Shows exceptional race survival and composure when team plans collapse (2 observations)
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 role upgrade to precision launch-point pacing for Evenepoel suggests increased trust and tactical sophistication compared to earlier general domestique duties
